scope_exclusions: training shared models on private artifacts

parallelism_policy.parallel_allowed_after: schema, artifact, and CLI boundaries are stable

workstreams.name: CLI, Command Envelope, And Artifact Contracts
Live Verify, Cleanup, Boundaries, CI, Share

initial_slice.objective: Build the deterministic Go CLI skeleton, command-result envelope, exit-code constants, config path handling, and tests.
initial_slice.reason: This creates the executable shell for later PRD acceptance work without requiring live credentials.

notes: T2.5 establishes the repository delivery lifecycle baseline before T3 product implementation.
T9 remains the product-facing Lumyn CI/JUnit/PR-comment feature required by the MVP.
T2.6 establishes dev/architecture propagation and CodeQL scanner posture before T3 product implementation.
T2.5 now also establishes required-check metadata, CODEOWNERS owner-review coverage, workflow permissions/concurrency/timeouts, pinned toolchain setup from go.mod, and action-ref exception posture.
Required-check metadata must stay aligned with branch protection and commit-push required checks before autoship or merge.

runtime.dependency_policy: standard library only for T1; add pinned dependencies only when required by later tasks

notes: T2.6 maps dev and architecture guide requirements into future product task packets before T3.
CodeQL is delivery risk-lane infrastructure and remains separate from the product-facing T9 GitHub Action/JUnit/PR-comment feature.
T2.6 now propagates CI lanes, docs parity, output contracts, release integrity, provenance, systems-thinking, TDD/ADR, performance, reliability, and fail-closed requirements before T3.
All current task packets carry inherited guide refs and planning-skill fields; future task packets must do the same or record an approved exception.
T2.6 now also enforces Factory prd-to-plan and execution-compiler story fields before T3 product implementation.
T2.6 now enforces Factory compatibility, alignment-gate refs, plan-drift refs, explicit scope exclusions, canonical worker chains, and lifecycle gates on every task packet.
Runtime pins now preserve the current Factory profile shape as well as Lumyn-specific Go aliases.
T2.6 now propagates coverage gates and coverage exceptions from docs/dev/dev_guides.md before T3 product implementation.
Future CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle tasks must preserve the required CI control set or record an approved exception before implementation continues.

release: reserved until standalone binary release packaging starts

engineering_policies.docs_parity: Behavior, flags, outputs, install paths, and workflow semantics update README, PRD, dev/architecture guides, and Factory planning artifacts together.
engineering_policies.output_contracts: Command-result envelope, exit codes, schemas, cassettes, evidence, reports, and run artifacts are product contracts.
engineering_policies.release_integrity: Standalone binary release tasks must define version, changelog, install, artifact-integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.
engineering_policies.provenance_evidence: Validation reports, work-proof markers, PR lifecycle reports, scanner status, and scope closure evidence remain repo-relative.

architecture_policies.systems_thinking: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, deletion blast radius, rollback/deletion test, and source-of-truth impact.
architecture_policies.tdd: Behavior changes require test-first evidence or structured skipped reason; schema/output changes prefer failing fixture or validator expectation first.
architecture_policies.adr_triggers: Boundary crossings, public contracts, failure classes, state model, performance, security, credential/network, and release posture changes require ADR or decision note.
architecture_policies.performance: Replay/eval/report hot paths and model-cost paths require budget notes and validation or approved exception.
architecture_policies.reliability: Retries, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider work require hardening checks and failure-mode evidence.
architecture_policies.failure_semantics: Ambiguous proof, redaction, credential, stale cassette, undeclared control, or scanner state fails closed.

coverage_policy.exception_policy: coverage misses block promotion unless the task records an approved scoped exception with owner, reason, expiry or follow-up task, and compensating validation evidence

validated_rules: alignment decisions are resolved before implementation locks runtime, distribution, provider, artifact namespace, credential, live-work, or dependency choices
execution plan carries public API and contract map, docs/OSS readiness baseline, test matrix wiring, minimum-now sequence, explicit non-goals, and definition of done

task packets carry runtime pins, slice rationale, changelog/versioning intent, contract/API impact, architecture constraints, ADR posture, TDD-first tests, cost/perf impact, chaos/failure hypothesis, and semantic invariants
guide propagation from docs/dev/dev_guides.md and docs/architecture/architecture_guides.md is enforced before T3 product implementation
public or executable contract-impact tasks must be ADR-gated with adr_required=true
execution plan, validation contract, context brief, and task packets carry factory_compatibility and block deprecated active worker aliases
task packets carry scope_exclusions, alignment_gate_ref, and plan_drift_policy_ref inherited from the execution plan
task packets carry allowed_paths and forbidden_paths before factoryd dispatch
runtime pins use the current Factory runtime-pins contract including toolchain_version, module_or_package_path, provider_policy, artifact_namespace, and live_work_policy
task packets that are runner-ready carry worker_type, factoryd_runtime, validation_commands, evidence_required, and stop_conditions before factoryd dispatch
task packets carry coverage_policy_refs for first-party code, tests, CI, package-boundary, and generated-code work
safety/corpus-ready evidence requirements are itemized in the acceptance ledger, mapped into task packets, and kept local/private by default
planning_skill_alignment.drift_policy: If runtime, distribution, provider, dependency, artifact namespace, credential, live-work, guide, validation-contract, Factory contract, profile, skill inventory, or skill vocabulary decisions change after planning, update context brief, execution plan, task packets, validation contract, and factory_compatibility before implementation continues, safety/corpus-ready evidence artifacts, and corpus posture artifacts before continuing.

stable_public_surfaces: CLI commands: lumyn init, check, record, verify, trace, demo, share, eval
command-result JSON envelope and exit-code constants
workflow, cassette, run, baseline, evidence, proof, redaction, report, JUnit, and share artifacts
GitHub Action, PR comment summary, JUnit output, and proof-honest badge
internal_surfaces: internal/ packages for config, schema, recording, replay, reporting, live verify, sharing, and eval
public_api_and_contract_map.schema_versioning_policy: Every machine-readable artifact carries schema_version; incompatible changes require versioning, migration notes, and scope-closure evidence before release.
public_api_and_contract_map.error_contract_policy: Ambiguous proof, redaction, credential, network, stale-cassette, scanner, or control states fail closed with structured command-result evidence.

docs_and_oss_readiness_baseline.docs_parity_rule: Behavior, flags, output shapes, exit codes, artifact paths, install paths, CI/review flow, and workflow semantics update docs and planning artifacts in the same change.
docs_and_oss_readiness_baseline.oss_readiness_rule: User-facing docs explain workflows before internals, publish proof strength honestly, and avoid claiming unsupported live, release, or cross-platform guarantees.
docs_and_oss_readiness_baseline.release_readiness_rule: Release-sensitive tasks must define version, changelog, install, artifact integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.

test_matrix_wiring.policy: Every task packet cites active/planned/reserved/blocked tiers or records an approved non-applicable reason.

minimum_now_sequence.why_now: Establish deterministic CLI/runtime shell before schema or product behavior.
Lock executable artifact contracts before source intake, record, replay, report, live, or eval behavior.
Establish delivery lifecycle, guide propagation, CodeQL, and planning-skill gates before T3 product implementation.
Close safety/corpus-ready local evidence contracts, then close deterministic record/contract/replay/report foundation without live credentials.
Add live verify, boundary, CI, and share behavior after deterministic proof foundation exists.
Add stochastic eval only after deterministic replay, live boundaries, and credential/network posture are approved.

explicit_non_goals: training shared models on private artifacts
Do not treat live eval pass rate as deterministic closure evidence.
Do not bypass PR lifecycle, CodeQL, branch protection, or latest-head Codex review evidence.
Do not add production credential brokering or hosted control-plane behavior inside the Lumyn MVP implementation plan.
Do not upload normalized failure evidence, traces, cassettes, or reports to a hosted corpus in the MVP.
Do not position Lumyn MVP as a broad agent security platform; surface action-boundary safety findings as evidence inside the workflow completion product.
definition_of_done: All MVP acceptance groups in docs/product/prd.md are implemented or explicitly accepted with documented delivery debt by a human owner.
Every task has validation report, work-proof marker, review/ship/PR lifecycle evidence when required, and scope-closure evidence.
make prepush-full passes locally and required GitHub checks, including validate and CodeQL analyze, are green or have approved exceptions.
Latest-head passive Codex review is approved, thumbs-up, actionable-resolved, carried forward, or explicitly excepted before merge.
No task writes outside allowed paths, introduces undeclared credentials/network, or does explicitly excluded work.
Docs, changelog/versioning intent, output contracts, architecture constraints, and planning artifacts stay aligned with implemented behavior.
Required-check metadata, owner-review coverage, workflow hardening, action-ref posture, and branch-protection expectations are proven or explicitly excepted for CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle work.

Reports, traces, JSON output, CI output, PR comments, and eval evidence distinguish completion gaps, proof gaps, boundary violations, safety/security relevance, redaction/share failures, stochastic failures, and fix targets.
Normalized local failure evidence is corpus-ready for future opt-in use while defaulting to corpus_eligible: false and no hosted upload.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

runtime_pins.dependency_policy: standard library first; pinned Go modules only when a task proves need and records justification
runtime_pins.provider_policy: OpenAI-compatible HTTP and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters; no model key or network in deterministic bootstrap
runtime_pins.artifact_namespace: .factory/artifacts/ for Factory evidence; product artifacts use workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/
runtime_pins.live_work_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and human approval unlocks sandbox/model/network posture

blocking_decisions: distribution: standalone binary first, Homebrew later, PyPI not primary
provider: OpenAI-compatible HTTP and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters for eval, no SDK-heavy dependency during deterministic bootstrap
artifact namespace: Factory evidence in .factory/artifacts/ and product artifacts in workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/
credential and live-work posture: no network, sandbox credentials, or model keys until deterministic replay passes and human approval unlocks them
dependency policy: standard library first; pinned dependencies only when required and justified

drift_triggers: Factory contract, profile, skill inventory, or skill vocabulary changes that alter required fields, lifecycle workers, validation gates, or evidence expectations

mvp_eval_provider_adapters.sdk_policy: no heavy SDK dependency for MVP bootstrap; use thin HTTP adapters unless a later task justifies a pinned SDK
mvp_eval_provider_adapters.network_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and human approval unlocks model/network posture

factoryd_runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
factoryd_runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
factoryd_runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

mvp_required_version_slices.name: Record, Contract, Replay, Report
mvp_required_version_slices.completion_policy: Required internal capability slice; not a standalone public release or MVP completion point.

mvp_required_version_slices.name: Live Known-Path Verify, Basic Boundaries, CI, And Share
mvp_required_version_slices.completion_policy: Required internal capability slice; live sandbox/network work remains gated by approved credential posture.

mvp_required_version_slices.name: Live Agent Eval
mvp_required_version_slices.completion_policy: Required internal capability slice; live eval dispatch remains gated by product-pull evidence and approved model/network posture.

mvp_version_slice_policy.rule: v0.0, v0.1, and v0.2 are legacy internal planning slices and all are required for full MVP closure.

delivery_slices.name: Live Known-Path Verify, Basic Boundaries, CI, And Share
